authoritative_sources.title: Comptroller's Handbook: Regulatory Reporting
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The OCC is a primary U.S. banking regulator and this handbook is its official public guidance for governance, controls, amendments, and management oversight of regulatory reporting.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Reporting governance, workpaper control, and amended-report decisioning

authoritative_sources.title: Instructions for Preparation of Consolidated Reports of Condition and Income (FFIEC 031 and FFIEC 041)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FFIEC Call Report instructions are the controlling public instructions for call report content, signatures, amendments, edit handling, and submission mechanics for insured depository institutions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Call Report line support, submission, edit resolution, and amended filing workflow

authoritative_sources.title: FR Y-9C: Consolidated Financial Statements for Holding Companies
authoritative_sources.publisher: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Federal Reserve is the authoritative public owner of FR Y-9C reporting requirements and instructions for top-tier holding companies and their consolidated financial statements.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Holding-company reporting scope, signatures, and filing package preparation

authoritative_sources.title: Financial Statement Filing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NAIC is the recognized public coordinator for insurer financial-statement filing mechanics, filing software expectations, and annual and quarterly statement submission workflow.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Insurance annual and quarterly statement filing preparation

authoritative_sources.title: Annual Financial Reporting Model Regulation (Model 205)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NAIC Model 205 is the recognized public baseline for insurer annual financial reporting controls, workpaper retention, and management internal-control reporting.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Control evidence, workpaper retention, and management-report support

authoritative_sources.title: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
authoritative_sources.publisher: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision / Bank for International Settlements
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: BCBS 239 is the recognized public standard for data accuracy, integrity, completeness, timeliness, adaptability, and governance in bank risk and regulatory reporting.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Data lineage, reconciliation, and reporting-control design review

workflow_stages.name: Reporting regime and filing-scope classification
workflow_stages.description: Determine the applicable report family, reporting entity, consolidation scope, reporting period, filing deadline, and amendment posture before any balance or line-support conclusion is made.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Line support, source traceability, and workpaper assembly
workflow_stages.description: Map general-ledger, subledger, policy, claim, payment, or servicing records into report schedules and assemble the workpaper package needed to support each line and adjustment.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Reconciliation, edit resolution, and variance explanation
workflow_stages.description: Match report balances to source systems, evaluate edit failures and unexplained variances, and determine whether unresolved differences require escalation instead of unsupported correction.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Control, review, and attestation-support preparation
workflow_stages.description: Map reporting controls, review evidence, signoff dependencies, and retention expectations into an audit-ready package without overstating attestation authority.

workflow_stages.name: Exception escalation, amendment, and refiling support
workflow_stages.description: Escalate unsupported adjustments, missing evidence, policy conflicts, fraud-sensitive facts, or regulator-facing interpretation questions and prepare amendment or refiling support when appropriate.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

artifacts: reporting calendars, filing deadlines, and amendment status records
general ledger trial balances and feeder-system balances
subledger extracts for loans, deposits, claims, policies, reserves, or payments
account-to-schedule mapping tables and reporting workpapers
Call Report, FR Y-9C, statutory statement, or comparable filing templates and prior submissions
edit-check outputs, variance explanations, and unresolved exception logs
approval history, reviewer signoff packets, and attestation-support artifacts
KYC, AML, fraud, dispute, complaint, and consumer-impact records when they change reporting posture
retention evidence, management review logs, and control-performance artifacts
systems_of_record: core banking, servicing, policy administration, claims, or payment systems
regulatory reporting software or XBRL preparation tools
fraud monitoring, AML case management, and compliance systems

allowed: Determine whether the retrieved records and source documentation support a reporting line, posting, adjustment, amendment package, or filing-readiness conclusion.
Prepare evidence-backed reporting-readiness summaries, reconciliation notes, control mappings, and escalation packets without overstating authority.
Route the work to the correct adjacent specialist, approver, or attestation owner when legal, accounting-policy, fraud, AML, or regulator-facing authority is required.
escalate: Required tenant facts, reporting-regime context, source records, or authoritative source context are missing and would change the outcome.
Fraud, AML, consumer-protection, accounting-policy, legal, or policy-interpretation issues materially affect the safe answer.
Reconciliation differences, edit failures, disclosure conflicts, or record-truth conflicts cannot be resolved from the retrieved source set.
refuse: Unsupported override, top-side adjustment, signoff bypass, or filing-readiness conclusion without source evidence and authority.
Requests for legal advice, final accounting-policy decisions, formal attestation, regulator-facing submission, or final financial approval.
Any request that requires fabricating reconciliations, workpapers, approvals, line definitions, or regulated advice.

domain_failure_modes: Treating deadline pressure as permission to force a filing, override edit checks, or book unsupported reporting adjustments.
Using stale or partial ledger and workpaper evidence to declare reporting readiness while material variances remain unresolved.
Confusing depository, holding-company, and insurance reporting regimes and therefore applying the wrong line instructions or signoff expectations.
Failing to distinguish reporting-package preparation from legal interpretation, accounting-policy signoff, or final attestation authority.
Collapsing fraud, AML, sanctions, or consumer-impact issues into generic reporting exceptions instead of escalating them.
Claiming control completion or data lineage sufficiency when retention, review, or mapping evidence is missing.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Disclosure or reconciliation conflict that requires escalation
Source records do not support the requested billing or adjustment
Reconciliation mismatch or denial requires exception handling
Stakeholder asks for an unsupported financial override
A required control is missing evidence
Normal in-scope execution with realistic terminology
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.details: Public baseline sources cover U.S. depository, holding-company, and insurer reporting workflows, but exact domestic-state supplements, legal-entity hierarchies, and vendor filing implementations remain tenant and jurisdiction inputs that must be retrieved before regime-specific conclusions are finalized.
